text: Mobile app development has become a major industry. One key concept is responsive design, which means the app adapts smoothly to different screen sizes, like phones and tablets. Developers often choose between building a native app, designed specifically for iOS or Android, or a cross-platform app, which works on both systems using a single codebase. Native apps typically offer better performance and access to device features, but they require more separate work. Cross-platform development is faster and cheaper, though it may not be as powerful. Finally, after coding and testing, the app is submitted to app stores, like Google Play or the Apple App Store, for users to download.
